sprint: Sprint 16 -- Wiring: make what we built actually run
spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-20-from-spool-session6-findings-and-directives.md + Ralph US-206 reconciliation + TD-029 CI gap + I-017 doc dedup + B-037 Pi Sprint US-175 carryforward
backlogItem: B-037 (Pi Sprint phase continued: Spool Session 6 drill findings + CIO Session 6 directives 1-3) + B-043 partial (US-216 power orchestrator, Spool-audit-gated) + TD-029 closure (US-213) + TD-019/020/021/022 close-audit (US-215) + I-017 closure (US-218)

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-15 close baseline: Ralph's Session 80+ handoff reports fast suite ~2806 (+201 over Sprint 15 baseline 2605, 0 regressions). Sprint 15 merged to main@b0461df on 2026-04-20.

sprintNotes: GOAL: wire what we built in Sprints 13-15 into a system that actually works in production. Every story either (a) fixes a Spool Session 6 finding, (b) consolidates Sprint 15 work, or (c) wires existing pieces into a runnable whole. Zero new feature surface beyond what CIO directives explicitly require.

EXECUTION ORDER (P0 first, gated stories slip-ok): US-210 (hotfix, S) -> US-211 (BT resilient, M) -> US-212 (data_source hygiene, S) -> US-213 (Alembic gate, M) -> US-214 (US-206 reconciliation, M) -> US-215 (TD 019-022 close-audit, S) -> US-217 (battery_health_log schema, S) -> US-218 (I-017 dedup, S) -> US-219 (review ritual, S) -> US-216 (power orchestrator, L, SPOOL-AUDIT-GATED).

SPRINT 16 GATE: US-216 (Power-Down Orchestrator, L) is EXPLICITLY GATED on offices/pm/inbox/<DATE>-from-spool-power-audit.md existing. Ralph MUST NOT start US-216 without that note. If audit lands early-sprint, Ralph picks up; if late, US-216 slips to Sprint 17 -- that's acceptable. US-217 (battery_health_log) can land independently.

THEME: post-Sprint 15, Pi data-collection surface is complete but not running reliably. Service is in --simulate, collector dies on BT drop, no graceful shutdown, benchtest rows pollute 'real' data, server migrations can recur the CI gap. Sprint 16 plugs every one of those leaks. After this ships, the next real drive CIO takes actually produces clean end-to-end data that lands on server, runs through analytics, produces a Spool review -- the full pipeline runs uncoached.

CONSTRAINTS: Session 23 regression fixture (data/regression/pi-inputs/eclipse_idle.db) stays FROZEN. Spool spec values in grounded-knowledge.md + obd2-research.md stay authoritative. Existing power-mgmt code in src/pi/power/ + src/pi/hardware/ups_monitor.py (~1530 lines) gets audited by Spool, not rewritten by Ralph. Simulator code stays in src/pi/obdii/simulator.py as developer-invokable, just not the default production path.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool Session 6 note (primary), Ralph US-206 reconciliation note, TD-029 file, I-017 file, TD-019/020/021/022 files, Spool benchtest-hygiene note, Spool BT-resilient note (amended in Session 6). PM Rule 7: every threshold, column, event_type, SOC value traces to one of these sources.

POST-SPRINT-16 UNBLOCKS: first real drive with full pipeline running (US-208 validator finally has something real to validate); monthly drain test cadence (US-217 + US-216 enable); future schema additions safely auto-apply (US-213); data_source filter stays a meaningful signal (US-212); drive-level analytics show one row per drive (US-214).

stories.title: Pi Collector Hotfix -- drop --simulate + Restart=always + journald persistent
stories.intent: Per CIO directive 1 (Spool Session 6): flip eclipse-obd.service off simulate mode and harden service restart. Three bundled changes to deploy/eclipse-obd.service and /etc/systemd/journald.conf. Without this, the collector runs simulate forever (Pi's been generating 352K synthetic 'real' rows since Sprint 14) and we cannot capture real OBD. Additionally archive sim code behind an explicit developer flag so it's not default production path. Lands first in Sprint 16 -- unblocks every other 'real-data' story.

filesToTouch: deploy/eclipse-obd.service (ExecStart: drop --simulate flag; Restart: on-failure -> always; preserve DISPLAY/XAUTHORITY env from US-196)
deploy/journald-persistent.conf (new drop-in or edit of /etc/systemd/journald.conf via deploy script: Storage=auto -> Storage=persistent)
deploy/deploy-pi.sh (new step to install journald-persistent drop-in + systemctl restart systemd-journald; under --init AND default flow since this is idempotent)
src/pi/main.py (ensure --simulate remains a DEVELOPER flag; no default activation; stdout banner when simulate is set so it's obvious)
docs/testing.md (new Developer Simulate Mode section: how to invoke for local testing; explicit warning that production service does NOT use it)
tests/deploy/test_eclipse_obd_service.sh (new or extend -- asserts ExecStart contains no --simulate flag, Restart=always, DISPLAY env present)
offices/pm/issues/ (no new issue; operational cleanup)
doNotTouch: Sim generator code itself (src/pi/obdii/simulator.py stays intact -- still used by developer flag + tests)
rfcomm-bind.service (unrelated unit, stays)
Pi system-level services beyond eclipse-obd.service + systemd-journald

/etc/systemd/journald.conf (or drop-in) has Storage=persistent; systemd-journald restarted; /var/log/journal exists after deploy.
deploy-pi.sh --init applies both changes atomically. Idempotent re-run is no-op.
Post-deploy: ssh Pi -> `systemctl cat eclipse-obd.service | grep ExecStart` shows no --simulate; `systemctl show eclipse-obd -p Restart` shows Restart=always.
Post-deploy: ssh Pi -> `journalctl --verify` reports clean. `ls /var/log/journal/` shows machine-id directory (persistent journal is live).
--simulate remains invokable manually via `python src/pi/main.py --simulate` for developer local testing; prints a prominent stdout banner when active.
tests/deploy/test_eclipse_obd_service.sh or equivalent asserts the ExecStart + Restart + journal-storage invariants (Windows-skipable shell test per existing pattern).

Inbox confirmation to offices/tuner/inbox/YYYY-MM-DD-from-ralph-us210-hotfix-complete.md with before/after systemd unit diff + post-deploy journalctl evidence.
verification: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'systemctl show eclipse-obd -p Restart -p RestartSec -p StartLimitBurst'

invariants: Do NOT delete src/pi/obdii/simulator.py. Developer path MUST remain available.
Do NOT rebuild rfcomm-bind.service or touch other Pi systemd units.
Do NOT change DISPLAY/XAUTHORITY/SDL_VIDEODRIVER environment -- preserve US-192 state.
Do NOT delete /var/log/journal contents during install -- persistent journal starts fresh via systemd, not via rm.
Restart=always is NOT a free pass for crash-looping -- StartLimitBurst cap at 10 over 300s preserves systemd's emergency stop.
--simulate dev flag MUST print a banner that includes 'SIMULATE MODE -- NOT FOR PRODUCTION' so operators never think it's production output.
stopConditions: STOP if /etc/systemd/journald.conf already has Storage=persistent set (idempotency check) -- do NOT rewrite. Move on.
STOP if systemctl daemon-reload fails after unit edit -- file inbox note with journalctl output; do NOT force.
STOP if post-deploy the service crash-loops > 3 times in 60s -- revert to previous unit content, file inbox note.
STOP if --simulate path has other code dependencies that break when removed as default (e.g., a test that assumes --simulate is on) -- file inbox note before dropping.

stories.title: BT-Resilient Collector -- reconnect loop + error classification + event types
stories.intent: Per Spool Session 6 drill: CIO's hypothesis confirmed that BT drop today kills the collector. The main capture loop must classify errors into ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE / ECU_SILENT / FATAL and stay alive across BT flaps. No systemd work (eclipse-obd.service exists; US-210 hardens its restart policy). Scope is the Python-side resilience: reconnect-wait loop with 1/5/30/60s backoff cap; new connection_log event_types for post-hoc drill analysis. Soft-blocker for US-208 real-drive validator to produce meaningful output.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator.py or runLoop (classify errors at capture boundary: ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE | ECU_SILENT | FATAL)
src/pi/obdii/obd_connection.py (expose clean disconnect + reconnect-probe primitives; reusable from reconnect loop)
src/pi/obdii/bluetooth_helper.py (add isRfcommReachable() probe: lightweight adapter liveness check without full OBD() constructor)
src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py (new -- backoff schedule [1, 5, 10, 30, 60, 60, ...], probe-based recovery, reset on successful reconnect)
src/pi/data/database_schema.py (extend connection_log.event_type enum / CHECK constraint with new values: bt_disconnect, adapter_wait, reconnect_attempt, reconnect_success, ecu_silent_wait)
src/pi/data/connection_logger.py (new event_type helpers so callers write canonical strings, not magic values)
specs/architecture.md (Section 5 or new Collector Resilience subsection: error taxonomy, reconnect loop behavior, connection_log timeline interpretation)
docs/testing.md (BT drop-resilience walkthrough: unplug OBDLink mid-capture, observe collector stays running + logs flap, replug, verify resume)
tests/pi/obdii/test_reconnect_loop.py (new -- Fake CommandRunner-style probe injection, backoff schedule validation, reset-on-success, FATAL raise-through)
tests/pi/obdii/test_error_classification.py (new -- canned exceptions classify into 3 buckets correctly, boundary cases documented)
tests/pi/data/test_connection_log_event_types.py (new -- CHECK constraint allows new enum values, writers emit canonical strings)
tests/pi/integration/test_bt_drop_resilience.py (new integration -- FakeObdConnection + simulated BT drop + verified collector PID unchanged)
filesToRead: offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-20-from-spool-pi-collector-resilience-story.md (earlier Spool version -- partially superseded; scope of Story 2)
src/pi/obdii/obd_connection.py (US-193 MAC resolver + current disconnect semantics)
doNotTouch: eclipse-obd.service (US-210 covers systemd; US-211 is Python-only)
Mode 01 polling tiers (US-199 scheduler stays -- resilience is orthogonal to poll cadence)
DTC event-driven trigger (US-204 MIL rising-edge stays -- resilience does not change event semantics)
Drive state machine (US-200 engine_state thresholds stay)

groundingRefs.value: Backoff schedule: 1s, 5s, 10s, 30s, 60s, 60s... (cap at 60s)
groundingRefs.source: Spool resilience note Section 'Reconnect-wait loop' + Session 6 amendment

groundingRefs.value: Error classes: ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE (OSError, rfcomm timeout), ECU_SILENT (rfcomm ok but no ECU response), FATAL (unexpected exception -- re-raise)
New connection_log event_types: bt_disconnect, adapter_wait, reconnect_attempt, reconnect_success, ecu_silent_wait
Probe via rfcomm reachability check (stat /dev/rfcomm0 + lightweight ATI), NOT full OBD() constructor
groundingRefs.source: Spool resilience note Section 'Reconnect-wait loop' 3rd bullet

groundingRefs.value: Process MUST NOT exit on BT disconnect; only FATAL surfaces to systemd for restart

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg --files-with-matches 'rfcomm|connect|OBD\(' src/pi/obdii/ to map all BT-touching sites. Record filesActuallyTouched.
New src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py exposes ReconnectLoop class with .waitForAdapter(probe, eventLogger) + .reset(); backoff schedule is [1, 5, 10, 30, 60, 60, 60, 60, ...] capped at 60.
Error classification function at the capture-loop boundary buckets raised exceptions into 3 classes. Fake exceptions in tests cover each class's dominant signatures.

On ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE: cleanly close python-obd connection (release self._connection), log bt_disconnect + adapter_wait to connection_log, enter ReconnectLoop; on successful probe, log reconnect_success, reopen OBD, resume.
On ECU_SILENT: stay connected; reduce poll cadence or pause (Ralph picks); log ecu_silent_wait; resume when ECU responds.
On FATAL: log with full traceback; re-raise (systemd handles via Restart=always from US-210).
connection_log schema CHECK constraint accepts the 5 new event_types; migration idempotent on Pi (extends, does not break existing rows).
Integration test: fake BT drop (mock rfcomm path missing) -> collector does NOT exit (ps assertion via FakeProcess wrapper or similar); probe returns success -> reconnect -> capture resumes.
Fast suite +N new tests, 0 regressions; ruff clean on all touched files; sprint_lint 0 errors.
specs/architecture.md Section 5 (or new Collector Resilience) documents error taxonomy + reconnect loop + event_type timeline.
docs/testing.md BT Drop-Resilience walkthrough: 5-step operator procedure with expected journalctl + sqlite3 evidence.
verification: ruff check src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py src/pi/obdii/orchestrator.py src/pi/obdii/obd_connection.py src/pi/obdii/bluetooth_helper.py
invariants: Process NEVER exits on BT disconnect. Only FATAL surfaces to systemd.
Reconnect backoff CAPS at 60s. Never exponential beyond cap; never aggressive polling.
connection_log event_types are ADDITIVE. Existing types (connect_attempt, connect_success, disconnect, drive_start, drive_end) stay valid.
No wake-on-BT, no heartbeat rows, no observability endpoint -- explicitly deferred to future story (per Spool scope constraint).
Probe primitive uses /dev/rfcomm0 stat + lightweight AT command -- NOT a full obd.OBD() reconstruction (expensive + stateful).
ECU_SILENT stays connected; do NOT tear down on engine-off -- that's normal behavior, not a disconnect.
stopConditions: STOP if BT error classification requires intercepting bluetoothd internals or kernel-level events. python-obd exception surface + /dev/rfcomm0 OSError should suffice; if not, scope creep -- inbox Spool.
STOP if the capture loop's current architecture tightly couples OBD connection to every PID poll (makes reconnect loop impossible to host cleanly) -- file inbox note with proposed refactor scope.
STOP if RestartSec + StartLimitBurst from US-210 interacts badly with the reconnect loop (e.g., systemd kills before reconnect completes) -- revisit the systemd config with Spool.
STOP if ECU_SILENT detection cannot be reliably distinguished from ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE on ISO 9141-2 (timeout shapes look similar) -- file inbox note with bus-trace evidence.

stories.title: Benchtest data_source hygiene -- explicit tagging on non-live-OBD writers
stories.intent: Close the root cause of US-205's 352K surprise. US-195 DEFAULT 'real' was intended as a safety net for the single live-OBD writer, but it also tags every benchtest / simulator / dev writer that doesn't pass data_source explicitly. Result: filter WHERE data_source='real' stops meaning 'from the real car.' Post-US-210 the simulate service is off, so the pollution pressure drops sharply, but the bug itself stays -- any future benchtest run or hidden dev writer recreates the mess. This story audits every non-live-OBD INSERT into capture tables and adds explicit data_source tagging.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/simulator.py (and any sibling simulator writers: pass data_source='physics_sim' on every INSERT into capture tables)
scripts/replay_pi_fixture.sh or src/pi/replay/ (replay path writes data_source='replay')
tests/pi/conftest.py or any test fixture loader that writes to operational DB (pass data_source='fixture')
src/pi/obdii/data/helpers.py::logReading (live-OBD path -- KEEP implicit DEFAULT 'real'; add a defensive assertion that data_source is not explicitly passed from a non-live caller)
src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py::logReading (same -- live-OBD path)
src/pi/data/database_schema.py (tighten docstring on data_source column: DEFAULT is a narrow safety net for the live-OBD writer ONLY)

specs/architecture.md Section 5 (tighten data_source contract language: 'Writers outside the live-OBD path MUST pass data_source explicitly; DEFAULT real is a narrow safety net for the single live-OBD collector, not a catchall for dev writers.')
tests/pi/data/test_data_source_hygiene.py (new -- grep-style AST scan asserts every non-live INSERT into capture tables passes data_source explicitly; runtime assertion catches violations)
filesToRead: specs/architecture.md Section 5 (current data_source contract language, line ~577)
doNotTouch: data_source column DEFAULT value -- stays 'real' (it is correct for live-OBD writers)
CHECK constraint enum values -- all 4 values (real, replay, physics_sim, fixture) remain valid
Existing operational rows -- NO data migration; US-205 already reset state, new writes land correctly going forward
Server-side data_source column (post-US-209 the server matches Pi; same DEFAULT, same CHECK -- hygiene scope is Pi-side writers)

stories.title: TD-029 close -- deploy-time server migration gate (Alembic or explicit)
stories.intent: Close the CI gap that bit US-205: SQLAlchemy model changes in src/server/models/ do NOT propagate to live MariaDB because CI tests only against ephemeral SQLite. US-209 applied the missing migrations one-shot, but the underlying class-of-bug recurs any time someone adds a server-side column. This story wires a deploy-time migration gate so future Sprint-14-style schema additions auto-apply on deploy-server.sh. Two viable approaches: (1) adopt Alembic as canonical (`alembic upgrade head` runs at deploy time); (2) keep per-feature migration scripts but add an explicit 'run migrations' step in deploy-server.sh --init and default flow. Ralph picks based on codebase fit; TD-029 captures both as options.

filesToTouch: src/server/migrations/ or src/server/alembic/ (new migration infrastructure: either Alembic init + versions/ OR explicit Python migration registry)
scripts/apply_server_migrations.py (enhanced: discovers + runs all registered migrations in order; idempotent per-migration; records applied migrations in a schema_version table)
deploy/deploy-server.sh (new step step_apply_migrations under --init AND default flow: runs apply_server_migrations.py --execute; fails deploy if migrations fail)
src/server/models/__init__.py (if Alembic chosen: integrate env.py with existing Base metadata; if explicit chosen: no changes needed)
specs/architecture.md (Server Schema Migrations subsection: deploy-time gate, adding new migrations, rollback notes)
docs/testing.md (developer workflow: how to write a new migration when adding a column; how to test migration on a scratch DB)
tests/server/test_migrations.py (new -- applies each migration on a fresh MariaDB-like shape, asserts idempotency, asserts schema_version advances correctly)
tests/deploy/test_deploy_server_migration_gate.sh (new -- asserts deploy-server.sh fails when migration fails; passes when migrations succeed)
offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-029-server-migrations-not-auto-applied.md (annotate Closed with chosen approach + rationale + rejected alternatives)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/data_source.py + drive_id.py (Pi-side ensure* migration pattern -- the same safety properties transfer)
pyproject.toml (check if alembic is already a dep; add if needed)
doNotTouch: Pi-side migrations (Pi already has ensure* idempotent migrations; scope is server-side only)
Existing applied migrations (US-209 state stays -- new story adds forward-looking gate, not retroactive replay)
Raw SQL data migrations or backfills -- scope is DDL only (schema), not data

stories.title: US-206 dual-writer reconciliation -- one DriveSummary row per drive
stories.intent: US-206 shipped drive-metadata capture via a dual-writer pattern (analytics writer keyed by (device_id, start_time) + Pi-sync writer keyed by (source_device, drive_id)) to avoid scope creep into analytics code. Result: two rows per drive on server, downstream consumers must UNION-join. Per Ralph's inbox proposal Option 1: converge on one row per drive. Pi-sync creates the row with drive_id + source_device + metadata; analytics updates the same row (found via (source_device, drive_id)) with start_time/end_time/duration/row_count/is_real. Requires _ensureDriveSummary to know drive_id at analytics-run time -- which it does (server has received drive_id via sync since US-200).

filesToTouch: src/server/services/analysis.py::_ensureDriveSummary (reshape to find-or-create by (source_device, drive_id); if row exists with metadata from Pi-sync, UPDATE with analytics-computed fields; if not, INSERT with both)
src/server/api/sync.py (DriveSummary sync path: ensure upsert by (source_device, drive_id) UNIQUE key is correct semantics post-reconciliation)

src/server/models/__init__.py::DriveSummary (tighten nullability: after reconciliation, start_time and device_id should be NOT NULL for analytics-written rows; Pi-sync rows temporarily NULL start_time until analytics runs at drive-end)
src/server/reports/drive_report.py (if this consumer was previously UNION-joining two rows, simplify to single-row query)
src/server/analytics/calibration.py (update is_real=True filter logic if needed -- Pi-sync row has is_real NULL initially; analytics sets it at drive-end)
src/server/analytics/advanced.py (DriveStatistic join path -- verify it resolves to the single reconciled row, not one of two)
scripts/reconcile_drive_summary.py (new -- one-shot migration to merge existing dual rows; find matching pairs via timestamp overlap + device_id; MERGE analytic-only row into pi-sync row; idempotent)

Analytics runs at drive-end: finds the Pi-sync-created row, UPDATE-populates analytics fields. No new row created.

Reconciliation migration script merges existing dual rows on current server: scan analytics-only rows (device_id + start_time present, source_device NULL); find matching Pi-sync rows (source_device + drive_id present, start_time NULL); MERGE analytic into pi-sync (backward-compatible with ID references). Idempotent.

Integration test: drive completes Pi-side -> Pi sync -> server has 1 row (metadata populated, analytics NULL). Analytics runs -> same row UPDATE (analytics populated). Final state: 1 row, both halves populated.
drive_report generates single-row-per-drive output; calibration sees is_real=True on reconciled rows; advanced analytics joins resolve to one row.

invariants: One row per (source_device, drive_id) after reconciliation. Existing UNIQUE constraint enforces.
Pi-sync writer order: drive_id + source_device + metadata FIRST; analytics fields populated later. Null analytics == drive-in-progress, expected.
Analytics writer: find-or-create semantics. Do NOT insert blindly -- MUST check by (source_device, drive_id) first.
Reconciliation migration is IDEMPOTENT -- re-running is no-op (no duplicate merges, no data loss).
is_real stays TRUE only when analytics has confirmed (drive-end). Pi-sync rows pre-analytics have is_real NULL.
Do NOT rename DriveSummary table (that was rejected in US-206 as too invasive; stays drive_summary).

stories.title: Power-Down Orchestrator -- staged 30/25/20 SOC shutdown + battery health log
stories.pmSignOff: Marcus 2026-04-20: L-size justified. Three CIO directives combine in one story: (2) staged shutdown ladder, and supporting pieces for directive 3 (monthly drain tests need battery_health_log). Scope: wire existing src/pi/power/ + src/pi/hardware/ infrastructure into the orchestrator loop + implement the 30/25/20 ladder + create battery_health_log table + one-row-per-drain-event writer. EXPLICITLY GATED on Spool audit deliverable -- the existing ~1530 lines of power-mgmt code may already implement parts of this. If Spool audit lands before Ralph hits this story in the queue, Ralph picks up; if audit is late, story slips cleanly to Sprint 17. No code written until Spool audit is in Ralph's inbox. Signed off for execution under the explicit-gate constraint.
stories.intent: Per CIO directive 2 (Spool Session 6): implement staged shutdown at warning 30% / imminent 25% / trigger 20% SOC. Without this, UPS drain test showed Pi hard-crashes at zero (no graceful ordering), orphan cleanup on next boot, potential data loss. Per Spool's amended scope: this story ALSO creates battery_health_log table + one-row-per-drain-event writer to enable monthly drain test analysis (CIO directive 3). EXPLICITLY GATED on Spool audit of existing src/pi/power/ + src/pi/hardware/ups_monitor.py (~1530 lines) -- if audit finds parts already implemented, story scope narrows to wiring. If audit finds nothing usable, full implementation. Ralph does NOT start this story until Spool's audit inbox note exists.

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (new OR extension of existing PowerManager: staged-shutdown state machine, consumes UpsMonitor.getPowerSource() + SOC, fires warning/imminent/trigger at 30/25/20)
src/pi/power/power_db.py (add battery_health_log write path if table schema is new; otherwise just new event_type for drain-complete)

src/pi/data/database_schema.py (new SCHEMA_BATTERY_HEALTH_LOG: drain_event_id INTEGER PK, start_timestamp TEXT canonical, end_timestamp TEXT NULL, start_soc REAL, end_soc REAL NULL, runtime_seconds INTEGER NULL, ambient_temp_c REAL NULL, load_class TEXT DEFAULT 'production', data_source TEXT DEFAULT 'real'; IX on start_timestamp)
src/pi/data/database.py (ensureBatteryHealthLogTable migration; idempotent)
src/pi/hardware/shutdown_handler.py (wire: on trigger@20%, close drives cleanly, push any queued sync, systemctl poweroff)
config.json (new pi.power.shutdownThresholds section: {warningSoc: 30, imminentSoc: 25, triggerSoc: 20}; defaults set, overridable)
specs/architecture.md (new Section Power-Down Orchestrator: staged ladder, thresholds, battery_health_log schema, integration with UpsMonitor + ShutdownHandler, failure modes)
docs/testing.md (UPS drain test drill protocol: monthly schedule, expected observations, battery_health_log inspection query)
tests/pi/power/test_shutdown_orchestrator.py (new -- state machine transitions at 30/25/20, hysteresis if SOC briefly re-crosses, callback firing)
tests/pi/power/test_battery_health_log.py (new -- schema + writer + idempotent migration + canonical timestamps)
tests/pi/integration/test_staged_shutdown_drill.py (new -- simulated UPS drain with mocked SOC values; assert transitions + poweroff call)

UpsMonitor.getPowerSource() inference logic (US-184 CRATE + VCELL-slope stays)
Drive state machine (US-200 stays)
BT connection handling (US-211 territory)
PowerManager callback API surface (extend, do not redefine)

groundingRefs.value: Staged shutdown thresholds: warning 30%, imminent 25%, trigger 20%
Stage behaviors: Warning (30%) flag DB + stop new drive_id + force sync push; Imminent (25%) stop OBD polling + close BT + force KEY_OFF; Trigger (20%) systemctl poweroff
groundingRefs.source: Spool Session 6 Section 'Story 3 (L, TBD)' Design decisions

groundingRefs.value: battery_health_log: one row per drain event (start SOC, end SOC, runtime, ambient if known, load class)
Battery-replacement alert when runtime drops >30% from baseline
UpsMonitor.getPowerSource() uses CRATE polarity + VCELL slope (US-184)
groundingRefs.source: existing code + US-184 + preserved in MEMORY.md

groundingRefs.value: Testable today via UPS drain (no car-accessory wiring needed)

acceptance: PRE-REQUISITE: offices/pm/inbox/<DATE>-from-spool-power-audit.md exists. Ralph does NOT start this story without it. If missing, file progress.txt entry 'US-216 gate not met; waiting on Spool audit; picking up next story' and skip.

Pre-flight audit: cross-reference Spool's audit findings with filesToTouch list. Narrow scope where existing code already implements a stage; expand only where genuinely missing. Record filesActuallyTouched.
Orchestrator state machine with 3 states (NORMAL, WARNING, IMMINENT) + TRIGGER terminal action. Transitions fire on SOC crossings (with hysteresis -- do NOT oscillate on 29.5 <-> 30.5).
At WARNING (30%): battery_health_log starts a drain event row; DB flag set to stop new drive_id minting; sync push scheduled if network available.
At IMMINENT (25%): OBD polling stops; BT connection closes cleanly; if a drive is active, force KEY_OFF with reason='power_imminent'.
At TRIGGER (20%): battery_health_log closes the drain event row (end_soc, runtime, end_timestamp); systemctl poweroff called; process exits cleanly.
battery_health_log schema matches groundingRef; migration idempotent; canonical timestamps via US-202 helper.
config.json pi.power.shutdownThresholds exposed; defaults {30, 25, 20}; overridable for dev testing (lower thresholds).
Drain test simulation: mocked UpsMonitor returns SOC values counting down 35->15 over a condensed interval; orchestrator fires all 3 stages + battery_health_log row populated correctly.

Real drain test (CIO-runnable): unplug wall power; observe journalctl for 'Shutdown WARNING at 30%' / 'IMMINENT at 25%' / 'TRIGGER: initiating poweroff at 20%' messages; battery_health_log on next boot shows complete drain event row.
specs/architecture.md + docs/testing.md updated with orchestrator + monthly drain test protocol.
Fast suite + new tests 0 regressions; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.
verification: (Real-drain verification CIO-driven) ssh Pi after drain + reboot: 'sqlite3 ~/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db "SELECT * FROM battery_health_log ORDER BY drain_event_id DESC LIMIT 1"'
invariants: Hysteresis on stage transitions: once WARNING fires at <=30%, do not de-escalate to NORMAL until SOC >=35% (5% band).
TRIGGER (systemctl poweroff) is a one-way action -- no recovery path; next boot starts fresh.
battery_health_log drain event MUST be closed (end_timestamp + end_soc written) before poweroff if possible -- INSERT on warning, UPDATE on trigger.
Use existing UpsMonitor.getPowerSource() + SOC reader; do NOT add new I2C reads.
Do NOT call poweroff anywhere except the trigger path. Do NOT call from a test (mock the sysctl call).
Config thresholds are dev-overridable but production-default is (30, 25, 20).
Hard invariant: from Spool directive: Pi NEVER runs to 0% on battery -- poweroff at 20% provides ~3-5 min safety margin on a healthy battery.
stopConditions: STOP if Spool audit inbox note not present at story start -- file progress.txt + skip to next story.
STOP if Spool audit reveals orchestrator already fully implemented (just not wired) -- narrow story scope to wiring + battery_health_log; file scope-narrow inbox note.
STOP if existing PowerManager.onBatteryPower callback fires during testing but nothing triggers poweroff -- that's a clue to the 'code exists but unwired' hypothesis; investigate before extending.
STOP if SOC threshold crossings trigger multiple times in a single drain (hysteresis gap) -- fix before closing.
STOP if integration test + real-drain test diverge (test passes but real drain doesn't fire stages) -- test is mocking too much; fix fidelity.

stories.title: Battery health tracking schema -- battery_health_log table + writer (Pi + server)
stories.intent: Split from US-216 so this can land independently if US-216 is gated on Spool audit and delayed. Creates battery_health_log table on Pi + server mirror; adds a simple writer entry point that can be called from existing UpsMonitor code. CIO directive 3 (monthly drain tests May-Sept) needs a place to land the test results -- this story gives that. US-216 then consumes the table when it wires the staged shutdown ladder. If US-216 ships in the same sprint, US-217 is absorbed into its pre-work; if US-216 slips, US-217 still lands and gives CIO immediate manual drain-test recording surface.

stories.intent: Close I-017 (filed Session 71 by Ralph) -- specs/standards.md and offices/ralph/agent.md currently carry overlapping content, leading to divergence risk + double-maintenance. Audit overlap, canonicalize source-of-truth locations, replace duplicated content with links + short summaries. specs/standards.md is project standard (Marcus owns); agent.md is Ralph's operational guide. Rule: facts that are project-wide standards live in specs/; agent-operational guidance lives in agent.md with 1-line 'per specs/standards.md' pointers where needed. Minor hygiene that improves every future agent onboarding.

stories.title: US-175 Spool data-quality review ritual -- wire report.py + prompts + checklist into runnable script
stories.intent: B-037 Pi Sprint carryforward: wire existing post-drive review artifacts (scripts/report.py, src/server/services/prompts/, Spool drill checklist) into one runnable script a CIO can invoke after any real drive. Doesn't implement new analysis -- purely glues the already-shipped pieces into a workflow. Fits the 'wire it together' sprint theme in its purest form. Output: scripts/post_drive_review.sh that takes a drive_id argument and walks through: (1) report.py summary for the drive, (2) Spool AI prompt invocation against the drive's data, (3) drill-checklist display, (4) a 'where to log findings' pointer.

Ollama model or server config
Drive state machine / drive_id generation

groundingRefs.value: scripts/report.py generates drive-keyed summary (Sprint 9 B-036 artifact)
src/server/services/prompts/ holds Spool AI prompt templates (plain-file load, Ollama invocation)
groundingRefs.source: MEMORY.md server tier + Spool 2026-04-16 prompts-delivered note

groundingRefs.value: Spool review flow: facts -> interpretation -> recommendations

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: confirm report.py + spool prompts + checklist file(s) all exist at expected paths. If any missing, file inbox note before building wrapper.
scripts/post_drive_review.sh accepts --drive-id <N> OR 'latest' (defaults to latest in drive_counter).
Script Step 1: runs report.py --drive-id N; captures output; prints with section header.
Script Step 2: invokes Spool prompt via scripts/spool_prompt_invoke.py; feeds drive data; prints Ollama response with section header. Gracefully handles 'insufficient data' response as valid.
Script Step 3: prints the drive-review-checklist.md content with section header.
Script Step 4: prints a 'where to record findings' pointer (e.g., offices/tuner/reviews/drive-N-review.md or an inbox note template).
Integration test: invoke with synthetic drive_id against test DB; assert script exits 0 + emits 4 sections; assert Ollama mock is called.
docs/testing.md Post-Drive Review Ritual documents the 5-step flow + CIO interaction points.
Inbox note to Spool: offices/tuner/inbox/YYYY-MM-DD-from-ralph-us219-review-ritual-ready.md so Spool knows the tool is available for next post-drive review.
Fast suite + new tests 0 regressions; shellcheck clean; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.
invariants: This story WIRES existing pieces. Do NOT write new analysis logic or new prompts.
Script fails gracefully when drive_id has no data (empty tables): prints 'no data for drive N', exits 0 (not 1 -- this isn't an error, it's a no-op).
Ollama call is invoked as configured in src/server/services/prompts/; do NOT hardcode model or endpoint -- read from config.json.
Review ritual does NOT mutate any data -- pure read + display.
Output formatting is plain text -- no fancy UI. CIO can cp/paste into a review note.
stopConditions: STOP if scripts/report.py doesn't accept --drive-id and adding it requires non-trivial refactor -- file inbox note with scope cost.
STOP if Spool prompt invocation requires modifying the prompt templates themselves -- Spool's lane, inbox note.
STOP if no drive-review-checklist.md exists at any reasonable path -- inbox Spool to provide one, defer Step 3.
